preamble: The development of the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] from an empty slot (often through a glide stage) is a classic example of fortition in the Romance languages.

examples.from: Latin initial [j] (phonetically close to ∅ in some contexts)
examples.note: Strengthening of initial palatal semi-vowels to affricates (e.g., 'jam' to Italian 'già').

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
